title: Slider
description: Slider lets users choose a numeric value within a range through direct manipulation. Use it for volume, thresholds, scales, and any setting where scrubbing is more natural than typing.
intro: Slider builds on the shared field system, so it supports labels, helper and error text, validation, disabled and readonly states, plus slider-specific features such as min/max ranges, step markers, vertical orientation, and formatted value text.

featureSections.title: Basic range selection
featureSections.description: Use a slider when users benefit from scrubbing through a continuous or semi-continuous range instead of entering exact values manually.

featureSections.title: Sizes and density
featureSections.description: Size changes the rail and thumb treatment so the control can fit compact tool panels or more prominent settings surfaces.

featureSections.title: Custom ranges and formatting
featureSections.description: Different ranges, steps, and formatted labels help adapt the slider to ratings, percentages, prices, and domain-specific scales.

featureSections.title: Discrete steps and min/max labels
featureSections.description: Show step markers when values are intentionally discrete, and show min/max labels when the scale needs visible anchors for interpretation.

featureSections.title: Vertical and readonly variants
featureSections.description: Vertical sliders work in dense side panels or media-style controls. Readonly is useful when the value should stay visible but not be changed in the current state.

featureSections.title: Settings panel pattern
featureSections.description: A realistic slider usually lives next to related settings, helper text, and feedback. This is more useful than showing isolated rails alone.

accessibility.title: Accessible name and value text
accessibility.body:
The slider uses `ariaLabel` when provided and otherwise falls back to the visible field label. Keep the label specific to the controlled setting, such as `Volume` or `Approval threshold`.

Use `ariaValueText` when the raw number is not descriptive enough, for example ratings, percentages, or formatted currency.

accessibility.title: Keyboard
accessibility.body:
| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| `ArrowLeft` / `ArrowDown` | decreases value by one step |
| `ArrowRight` / `ArrowUp` | increases value by one step |
| `Home` | jumps to minimum |
| `End` | jumps to maximum |
| `Tab` | moves focus to or from the slider |

accessibility.title: Range semantics and state
accessibility.body:
The input exposes `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, and `aria-valuenow`. Readonly sliders leave the value visible without allowing edits; disabled sliders are removed from normal interaction.

| State | Attribute |
| --- | --- |
| current value | `aria-valuenow` |
| min / max | `aria-valuemin` and `aria-valuemax` |
| richer wording | `aria-valuetext` when provided |
| readonly | `aria-readonly="true"` |

apiSections.title: SliderComponent - ui-slider
apiSections.lead: Standalone range input built on the shared FieldComponent. It integrates with Angular forms through ControlValueAccessor and adds slider-specific features such as vertical mode, step markers, formatted labels, and value semantics.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Discrete increment used for keyboard changes and snapping.
Optional unit string available for custom formatting scenarios.
Formats visible min and max labels when `showMinMax` is enabled.
Switches the slider to vertical orientation.
Provides a richer spoken value description than the raw number.
Displays visual notches for discrete steps.
Displays formatted min and max labels beside the rail.
Two-way slider value model used alongside ControlValueAccessor integration.

blocks.title: Inherited field inputs
rows: Shared field surface token inherited from `FieldComponent`.
Controls where the field label is placed relative to the control.
Supporting helper text rendered below the field when there is no active error.
Validation or manual error message managed by the shared field base.
Disables the field and its interactive affordances.
Keeps the value visible while preventing user changes.
HTML form name used by the field wrapper and native control wiring.
Control id used to connect label, help text, and error messaging.
Inherited autocomplete hint for native form compatibility.
Inherited shared-field clear-button flag when supported by the concrete control.
Overrides the accessible name when no visible label is appropriate.
Appends extra descriptive ids to the computed `aria-describedby` value.
Enables inherited automatic validation error handling from `FieldComponent`.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Emitted after committed value changes, including keyboard and pointer updates.

paragraphs: The component implements ControlValueAccessor, so it works with ngModel and Angular reactive forms.
Keyboard support includes arrow keys for step changes, Page Up and Page Down for larger jumps, and Home and End for min and max.
blocks.title: Notes
Related mechanisms
items: FieldComponent - shared base handling labels, helper and error text, aria wiring, and validation synchronization.
Native range input - provides the underlying interaction model and keyboard semantics.
